name: Mak-yek
author: AGP (traditional Thai/Cambodian, also Apit-sodok)
description: Mak-yek (Thai/Cambodian, also Apit-sodok) on an 8x8 board. Each player has 16 identical men, laid out on the first and third rank from each player's side. Every man moves like a rook (any number of empty squares orthogonally, no jumping). Two capture modes, both active on the mover's turn: CUSTODIAL — moving a man so one or more contiguous enemy men are flanked between it and a friendly man removes them; INTERVENTION — moving a man into the single empty square between two enemy men captures both. Moving into a sandwich is safe (capture is the mover's). Win by capturing all enemy pieces.
